original_query: select sum(l_extendedprice) / 7.0 as avg_yearly from lineitem, part where p_partkey = l_partkey and p_brand = 'Brand#35' and p_container = 'LG PKG' and l_quantity < ( select 0.2 * avg(l_quantity) from lineitem where l_partkey = p_partkey );
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_part AS (
    SELECT p_partkey
    FROM part
    WHERE p_brand = 'Brand#35' AND p_container = 'LG PKG'
),
part_avg AS (
    SELECT l_partkey, 0.2 * AVG(l_quantity) AS avg_q
    FROM lineitem
    WHERE l_partkey IN (SELECT p_partkey FROM filtered_part)
    GROUP BY l_partkey
)
SELECT SUM(l_extendedprice) / 7.0 AS avg_yearly
FROM filtered_part fp
JOIN lineitem l ON fp.p_partkey = l.l_partkey
JOIN part_avg pa ON l.l_partkey = pa.l_partkey
WHERE l.l_quantity < pa.avg_q;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace the correlated subquery with CTEs to precompute the average quantities only for the relevant partkeys
Filter the part table first to get only the relevant partkeys before computing averages
Use explicit JOIN syntax instead of implicit joins in the WHERE clause

original_query: select sum(l_extendedprice) / 7.0 as avg_yearly from lineitem, part where p_partkey = l_partkey and p_brand = 'Brand#32' and p_container = 'WRAP PKG' and l_quantity < ( select 0.2 * avg(l_quantity) from lineitem where l_partkey = p_partkey );
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_part AS (
    SELECT p_partkey
    FROM part
    WHERE p_brand = 'Brand#32' AND p_container = 'WRAP PKG'
), avg_quantities AS (
    SELECT l_partkey, 0.2 * AVG(l_quantity) AS avg_qty
    FROM lineitem
    WHERE l_partkey IN (SELECT p_partkey FROM filtered_part)
    GROUP BY l_partkey
)
SELECT SUM(l.l_extendedprice) / 7.0 AS avg_yearly
FROM filtered_part p
JOIN avg_quantities a ON p.p_partkey = a.l_partkey
JOIN lineitem l ON p.p_partkey = l.l_partkey
WHERE l.l_quantity < a.avg_qty;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replaced the correlated subquery with a CTE to avoid repeated calculations
Used a filtered_part CTE to pre-filter parts before joining
Optimized the join order to reduce the number of rows processed

original_query: select s_name, s_address from supplier, nation where s_suppkey in ( select ps_suppkey from partsupp where ps_partkey in ( select p_partkey from part where p_name like 'burnished%' ) and ps_availqty > ( select 0.5 * sum(l_quantity) from lineitem where l_partkey = ps_partkey and l_suppkey = ps_suppkey and l_shipdate >= date '1996-01-01' and l_shipdate < date '1996-01-01' + interval '1' year ) ) and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_name = 'GERMANY' order by s_name;
rewritten_query:
SELECT DISTINCT s_name, s_address
FROM nation
JOIN supplier ON supplier.s_nationkey = nation.n_nationkey
JOIN partsupp ON supplier.s_suppkey = partsupp.ps_suppkey
JOIN part ON partsupp.ps_partkey = part.p_partkey AND part.p_name LIKE 'burnished%'
JOIN (
    SELECT l_partkey, l_suppkey, 0.5 * SUM(l_quantity) AS half_sum
    FROM lineitem
    WHERE l_shipdate >= DATE '1996-01-01'
      AND l_shipdate < DATE '1997-01-01'
    GROUP BY l_partkey, l_suppkey
) AS lineitem_sum
ON partsupp.ps_partkey = lineitem_sum.l_partkey
   AND partsupp.ps_suppkey = lineitem_sum.l_suppkey
WHERE partsupp.ps_availqty > lineitem_sum.half_sum
  AND nation.n_name = 'GERMANY'
ORDER BY s_name;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replaced correlated subquery with a pre-computed aggregation in a derived table
Converted IN subqueries to explicit JOIN operations
Added DISTINCT to ensure each supplier appears only once in the result
Moved filter conditions into JOIN clauses for early filtering
Replaced date arithmetic with explicit date constant '1997-01-01'

original_query: select s_name, s_address from supplier, nation where s_suppkey in ( select ps_suppkey from partsupp where ps_partkey in ( select p_partkey from part where p_name like 'burlywood%' ) and ps_availqty > ( select 0.5 * sum(l_quantity) from lineitem where l_partkey = ps_partkey and l_suppkey = ps_suppkey and l_shipdate >= date '1994-01-01' and l_shipdate < date '1994-01-01' + interval '1' year ) ) and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_name = 'ETHIOPIA' order by s_name;
rewritten_query:
WITH lineitem_sum AS (
    SELECT l_partkey, l_suppkey, 0.5 * SUM(l_quantity) AS half_sum
    FROM lineitem
    WHERE l_shipdate BETWEEN DATE '1994-01-01' AND DATE '1994-12-31'
    GROUP BY l_partkey, l_suppkey
)
SELECT s_name, s_address
FROM supplier
JOIN nation ON s_nationkey = n_nationkey AND n_name = 'ETHIOPIA'
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM partsupp
    JOIN part ON partsupp.ps_partkey = part.p_partkey AND part.p_name LIKE 'burlywood%'
    JOIN lineitem_sum ON partsupp.ps_partkey = lineitem_sum.l_partkey 
        AND partsupp.ps_suppkey = lineitem_sum.l_suppkey
    WHERE partsupp.ps_suppkey = supplier.s_suppkey
    AND partsupp.ps_availqty > lineitem_sum.half_sum
)
ORDER BY s_name;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Precomputed the lineitem sum in a CTE to avoid recalculating it for each row in partsupp
Replaced nested IN subqueries with EXISTS and explicit JOINs for better performance
Used BETWEEN for date range instead of separate comparisons
Calculated the date '1994-01-01' + interval '1' year as '1994-12-31'
